_comment: SPECIALIST FLEET — three models GENUINELY DIFFERENT IN KIND, not three generalists from three labs. Prior fleets were peer generalists (Qwen3-32B/Gemma-3-27B/Mistral-24B), and every null (judge, ensemble, router) was measured on THAT homogeneity. This fleet swaps in real specialists so the Phase-1 gate can ask, with the QUERIES HELD FIXED (the frozen 30 hard queries), whether genuine specialization produces the divergence the peer fleet lacked. HARDWARE: 3x H200-141GB (NOT H100, NOT A100). The coder is FP8 block-wise quant needing compute cap >8.9 (Hopper/Ada), so A100 (8.0) is out — but ALSO, measured 2026-07-17, the coder weights are 74.9GB (NOT the earlier ~52GB guess): at mem_util 0.9 an 80GB H100 has NEGATIVE KV cache (-6.67 GiB) and CANNOT serve it, even 0.97 + fp8 KV is -1.12 GiB. So an 80GB H100 is TOO SMALL; this fleet REQUIRES H200 (141GB), one card each (~35-75GB each, CANNOT co-reside). 3x H200 secure ~$13.17/hr. DRIVER LOTTERY hits H200 too — EUR datacenters shipped driver 570/CUDA12.8 (too old for vLLM 0.24); force a US datacenter (US-GA-2 shipped 580/CUDA13). DEVICE PINNING: each model gets its own card via the `device` field in boot.sh (0-based CUDA index). Ports avoid 8001 (RunPod proxy squats on it). boot.sh checks nvidia-smi FIRST, re-roll if CUDA<13 or cards short. This is the coder axis: Qwen3-Coder-Next FP8 (80B total / 3B active MoE, non-thinking so NO <think> confound here).
name: coder

_comment: REASONING specialist — dense 32B, RL-reasoning-tuned, long chain-of-thought. THE <think> LANDMINE: this model emits a <think>...</think> block BEFORE its answer and the handoff already lost a session to exactly this (qwen3 max_tokens=1024 let the think block eat the budget and truncated the graded answer). So: max_len 16384 (room to think AND answer), and the harness must grade the text AFTER </think>, not the raw stream. If BF16 weights (~64GB) + KV do not fit 80GB at mem_util 0.9, drop to a 4-bit AWQ quant of the same model — that is the only expected OOM here. FLAG NOTE: `--reasoning-parser deepseek_r1` alone is correct for vLLM 0.24 (the old `--enable-reasoning` was removed); it puts the CoT in message.reasoning/reasoning_content and leaves the clean answer in message.content, which is what the harness grades. If the boot lands on the CUDA-12 vllm-0.11 fallback and rejects the flag, boot.sh fails CLOSED (kills the pod), so a bad flag wastes a boot but never serves silently-wrong data.
name: reasoning

_comment: GENERAL specialist — a broad-knowledge instruct model, genuinely NOT code- or math-specialized, as the contrast axis. MATCHED-STRENGTH + LINEAGE-DIVERSE: a 70B Meta-Llama, not a mid-size model, so this axis is comparable in strength to the 80B-MoE coder and 32B reasoner — a weak general model would just get DOMINATED and read as 'no specialization' (a false negative). Crucially it is META lineage: the coder is Qwen and the reasoner is Qwen-based (R1-Distill-QWEN), so a Qwen general would make 3/3 Qwen and dent decorrelation (the handoff's standing warning). Llama-3.3-70B is a third architecture family. AWQ-INT4 ~35GB fits one 80GB card with KV headroom; runs on Ampere OR Hopper (AWQ, not FP8), so it is not the card constraint — the coder's FP8 is. extra_args pins `--quantization awq` explicitly. max_len is 16384 (NOT 8192): candidate generation requests max_tokens=8192, and prompt + 8192 completion overflows an 8192 context -> HTTP 400 on every real query (measured 2026-07-17); the other two models were already 16384. UPGRADE (full-program only, NOT the gate): GLM-5.2 is the open frontier generalist but is 744B/40B-active MoE needing 4-5x 80GB cards + tensor-parallel — an order of magnitude heavier, out of scope for a 3-card gate.
name: general
